data.narrative:
Type One Energy is a Series B nuclear energy company founded by David Anderson, Chris Hegna, and John Canik, operating within a frontier-tech sector comprising 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, and Aalo Atomics.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, the only applicable triangulation approach given data constraints. Using the company's $100M+ total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 1.9x multiplier for Series B progression yields an implied valuation of $190M. This multiplier reflects typical venture step-ups between early and mid-stage rounds. A peer-median approach was not feasible; while 4 same-sector companies exist, none met the threshold of 3+ comparable datapoints when filtered by stage, timing, and valuation tier. Relying on broader-sector median comparison would introduce unwarranted noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $162M to $218M, centered on $190M. This range reflects approximately ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression point estimate. Key uncertainties include the precise post-money valuation from the $100M+ raise, the specific timing and structure of the most recent financing round, and the applicability of standard venture step-up multiples to a capital-intensive, regulated frontier sector where development timelines and technology risk may diverge from software comps. The single-method triangulation limits confidence; additional peer discovery in comparable-stage private nuclear ventures would strengthen this assessment.
